ModMetaData.name: RimSynapse - StoryTeller
ModMetaData.author: RimSynapse Team
ModMetaData.description:
[h1]RimSynapse - StoryTeller[/h1] [b]📚 [url=https://github.com/RimSynapse/RimSynapse-StoryTeller/wiki]Official Wiki & Documentation[/url][/b]
[b]An AI-Driven Storyteller That Learns Your Colony's Story.[/b]
Aura Algorithm is not just a difficulty slider â€” she is a narrative intelligence engine. She observes your colony, rewrites quest flavor text to match your story, and decides when factions attack based on what they actually know about you â€” not omniscient wealth calculations.
[b]NOTE: Aura requires an active LLM connection to orchestrate her narrative intelligence. If the LLM becomes disconnected or unresponsive, her storytelling logic will automatically fall back to Classic Cassandra.[/b]
[h2]Core Systems[/h2] [list] [*] [b]Dynamic Pacing Engine:[/b] The LLM continuously adjusts a global pacing multiplier that controls how frequently events fire. If your colony is recovering from a devastating raid, Aura will dial back the pressure. If things have been too quiet, she ramps it up. [*] [b]LLM-Weighted Incident Selection:[/b] Each incident category (ThreatBig, Misc, Disease, etc.) has a base weight that the LLM modifies in real-time. If you keep rescuing beggars, the AI boosts charity quest weights. If you're hoarding wealth, raid weights climb. [*] [b]Combat-Competency Threat Model:[/b] Vanilla RimWorld calculates raid size based on total colony wealth. Aura replaces this with a combat-competency model â€” raid strength is determined by your colonists' actual combat skills, weapon quality, and defenses. A colony rich in gold statues won't attract the same raid as one armed to the teeth. [*] [b]Quest Flavor Rewriting:[/b] When a new quest spawns, Aura intercepts it and rewrites the title and description via LLM to fit your current narrative arc, while preserving all gameplay mechanics (rewards, threats, timers). [/list]
[h2]Faction Perception System[/h2] [list] [*] [b]Subjective Knowledge:[/b] Factions no longer omnisciently know your wealth. Each hostile faction tracks a "Perceived Wealth" and "Perceived Strength" for your colony, updated only when their pawns visit or leave your map. [*] [b]Greed Ratio Raids:[/b] If a faction perceives you as wealthy but weak (high greed ratio), they become highly motivated to invade. This creates realistic escalation â€” early game you're not worth raiding; late game, you're a gold mine. [*] [b]Knowledge Propagation:[/b] When a trader or visitor leaves your map, they broadcast your colony's actual wealth and strength back to their faction. Allied factions share information more freely than hostile ones. [*] [b]Dynamic Arrival Modes:[/b] Industrial+ factions that are far away and highly motivated may drop-pod in rather than walk across the map. [/list]
[h2]Perception Window[/h2] [list] [*] [b]Faction Perceptions Tab:[/b] A new in-game window shows you exactly what each faction thinks your colony's wealth and strength are. Use this to strategize â€” or to panic. [/list]
[h2]Default Settings (internal, adjustable via XML)[/h2] [code] Incidents Target Days: 1.5 (Base days between incidents before LLM pacing) Global Pacing Multiplier: 1.0 (LLM-driven; >1 = more frequent events, <1 = fewer) Tension Modifier: 1.0 (LLM-driven; scales raid strength for narrative peaks) Greed Ratio Invasion Threshold: 3.0 (Perceived wealth/strength ratio that triggers raids) Knowledge Broadcast Cooldown: 60000 (Ticks between faction knowledge updates, ~1 day) [/code]
[h2]Requirements[/h2] [list] [*] [b]Harmony[/b] [*] [b]RimSynapse Core[/b] (Must be loaded before this mod) [*] A running local LLM backend via LM Studio or Ollama. [/list]
[h2]Support the Project[/h2] If you enjoy narrative AI storytelling, consider buying me a coffee! [url=https://buymeacoffee.com/archdukejim]â˜• Buy me a coffee[/url]
